f64_fixture_version: pending — fixture not yet generated (see f64_certification_status)
f64_certification_status: INFRASTRUCTURE READY, EVIDENCE PENDING (CERTIFY-BREADTH-1). The reusable F64 generator (tests/fixtures/generate_f64_reference.py) and the CPU-F32-vs-F64 harness (tests/certify_breadth_f64_validation_test.rs::gemma2_2b_atenia_f32_matches_f64, #[ignore]) are committed and wired. To fill the null drift below: (1) generate the fixture — `python tests/fixtures/generate_f64_reference.py models/gemma-2-2b-it tests/fixtures/gemma2_2b_reference` (needs ~21 GiB free RAM for the F64 pass); (2) run `GEMMA2_2B_DIR=models/gemma-2-2b-it cargo test --test certify_breadth_f64_validation_test --release -- --ignored gemma2_2b_atenia_f32_matches_f64 --nocapture`; (3) transcribe the printed max_abs_diff + argmax into certified_mode below. NO number is recorded until that run produces it. The certified_mode field additionally describes the GPU-TF32 kernel path; the harness measures the CPU-F32 path (ADR-004 primary metric) — a complementary, non-relaxed certification.
generated_on: RTX 4070 Laptop (sm_89), 8 GB VRAM, 32 GB RAM, Windows 11
config_compatibility_notes: Gemma 2 2B is the first non-Llama / non-Phi3 architecture certified by Atenia. Five architectural deltas from the Llama base: (1) **Dual-norm per layer** — four RmsNorms instead of two, with `post_attention_layernorm` and `post_feedforward_layernorm` applied to each sub-block's output BEFORE the residual add, while `pre_feedforward_layernorm` occupies Llama's `post_attention_layernorm` slot on the running stream. (2) **`(1+γ)·rms(x)`** RmsNorm instead of `γ·rms(x)`, folded into the loaded gamma at load time via `LoadTransform::AddScalar { scalar: 1.0 }` (M11.C step 3). (3) **SoftCap at two graph positions** — `cap=50.0` on pre-softmax attention scores (per-layer) and `cap=30.0` on final LM-head logits, realised by `NodeType::SoftCap` (M11.C step 2). Both are conditional on the matching config field being `Some(c)`. (4) **GeGLU FFN** — `gelu_tanh(gate)*up*W_down`. The pre-existing `nn_act::gelu` is the tanh-approximation Gemma 2 expects (`hidden_act = "gelu_pytorch_tanh"`); no new activation primitive needed. (5) **Embedding scale `× sqrt(hidden_size)`** = `× 48.0` for Gemma 2 2B, applied as a graph-level BroadcastMul against a `[1,1,1]` constant immediately after IndexSelect — cannot be folded into `embed_tokens.weight` because `lm_head.weight` is tied to it. Two further structural notes: (a) **`n_heads_q * head_dim ≠ hidden_size`** — Gemma 2 2B has `8 * 256 = 2048 ≠ 2304`. The Llama-family invariant assumed by `builder_shared.rs` does NOT hold; the Gemma 2 builder threads `q_dim = n_heads * head_dim` explicitly through every attention projection. (b) **Q-side scale folded into K** — Gemma 2 scales Q by `1/sqrt(query_pre_attn_scalar)` (256, equivalent to `1/sqrt(head_dim)` for 2B). The K-fold convention `Q*scale @ K^T = Q @ (K*scale)^T` keeps the mapper consistent with the Llama family while producing bit-identical output. **Sliding-window attention is deferred** — Gemma 2 alternates between sliding-window (window=4096, even-indexed layers) and full attention (odd-indexed). For any context < 4096 tokens the sliding-window is mathematically equivalent to full causal attention, so this builder ships full causal on every layer and matches the reference bit-exactly within the M11.C scope. Long-context support lands as a follow-up.

certified_mode.validation_notes: F64 reference not generated for Gemma 2 on the dev box. The 2.6B parameter footprint is structurally feasible for an F64 generation pass (peak F64 ~21 GiB, comfortably under the 32 GiB RAM ceiling), but the PyTorch F64 generation pipeline is not yet wired for Gemma 2 (the existing fixture covers the Llama M4.6 family only). Validation today: greedy-generation smoke under both modes, comparing decoded text — both modes produced bit-identical output on the M11.C reference prompt. SoftCap node was specifically validated by the M11.C step 2 `soft_cap_saturates_finite_no_nan` regression test (extreme inputs up to ±f32::MAX/2 with cap=50.0 produce finite saturated outputs); the smoke run produced no NaN or Inf in the logits.

fast_mode.validation_notes: Drift envelope inherited from ADR-005 family characterisation. Gemma 2 fast-mode argmax was bit-identical to certified-mode argmax on the M11.C reference prompt (single sample, 20 tokens). The two SoftCap node families (cap=50 on attention scores, cap=30 on logits) interact non-linearly with the BF16-TC drift, but the saturation regime caps both perturbation directions at `±cap`, so the output is bounded even in the worst-case drift scenario. Operators using Gemma 2 for production should validate fast-mode drift on representative prompts; the speedup over certified is only 1.32× on this sub-bracket (vs 6.84× for Phi-3.5 Mini), so the certified-mode default below is the right operating point unless an explicit speed/drift tradeoff has been characterised for the deployment.

smoke_validation.prompt: Tell me about the history of Rome
smoke_validation.certified_text:
## A Journey Through Time: The History of Rome

Rome's history is a sprawling tapestry woven
smoke_validation.fast_text:
## A Journey Through Time: The History of Rome

Rome's history is a sprawling tapestry woven
smoke_validation.interpretation: Bit-identical generated text under both modes on this prompt. Gemma 2 2B's fast/certified speedup (1.32×) is significantly lower than Phi-3.5 Mini's 6.84× on the same dev-box bracket — the gap is structural: Gemma 2 has 27 `NodeType::SoftCap` nodes per forward pass (26 per-layer attention caps + 1 final logit cap), each of which materialises its input tensor on CPU defensively (the executor calls `ensure_cpu` to handle BF16/CUDA inputs uniformly). The CPU↔GPU round-trips dominate the per-token cost: 26 × `[1, 8, S, S+C]` materialisations for attention scores plus one `[bs, 256000]` materialisation for the logits. The SoftCap forward kernel itself is trivial (elementwise tanh); the bottleneck is data motion, not compute. The 4.87 GiB BF16 model fits comfortably under the 8 GB VRAM ceiling (5.10 GiB resident under fast mode, 8.70 GiB under certified — the certified-mode F32 working tensors push more weight to RAM), so neither mode hits the disk tier. At 5.89 s/tok fast mode does NOT meet the M10 velocity target for the < 4B-param sub-bracket (target < 2 s/tok); a follow-up milestone landing a CUDA SoftCap kernel (eliminating the materialise-to-CPU pattern) would close the gap.

recommended_mode_rationale: Without an F64 reference for Gemma 2, the conservative default is certified. Fast mode produced bit-identical argmax in this single-prompt smoke and offers a 1.32× speedup; the per-tensor policy below routes the FFN tensors to fast (the family-wide structural heuristic) so most of the lift is captured without committing the whole model to the unverified-drift path. Operators wanting the modest additional fast speed can opt in via ATENIA_FAST_MODE=1 env override, with the explicit ADR-005 caveat that Gemma 2 fast-mode drift is uncharacterised at scale (especially relevant for the SoftCap interaction noted in the fast_mode validation_notes).
per_tensor_policy_status: Schema v2.0.0 (M11.B) — same family-wide structural heuristic as Mistral 7B / Falcon3 7B / Llama 3.x / Phi-3.5 Mini / the M4.6 fixture: FFN gate/up/down on fast, attention q/k/v/o on certified. The `*.mlp.*_proj.weight` pattern matches `gate_proj.weight`, `up_proj.weight`, and `down_proj.weight` (all three FFN linears); the four per-layer RmsNorm gammas and the four attention projections stay on certified. The K-side `1/sqrt(query_pre_attn_scalar)` Scale fold and the four `AddScalar(1.0)` folds on the norm gammas happen at load time (in F32) regardless of dispatch mode; the per-tensor policy only steers the matmul kernel selection at forward time.

verification.command_certified: atenia generate --prompt 'Tell me about the history of Rome' --model models/gemma-2-2b-it --max-tokens 20
verification.command_fast: ATENIA_FAST_MODE=1 atenia generate --prompt 'Tell me about the history of Rome' --model models/gemma-2-2b-it --max-tokens 20
verification.expected_text_prefix: ## A Journey Through Time: The History of Rome
verification.expected_architecture_log: [ATENIA] Architecture: Gemma2ForCausalLM — routing to gemma2 builder
